gods.description: Primeval Sumerian sea-goddess; engendered heaven (An) and earth (Ki). Mother of Enki.

gods.name: Ninlil
gods.description: Consort of Enlil; mother of Nanna. Followed Enlil to the underworld after his exile.

gods.name: Lahmu
gods.description: First generation of gods in the Enuma Elish; with Lahamu, parents of the cosmic horizon.

gods.name: Lahamu
gods.description: Female counterpart of Lahmu.

gods.name: Anshar
gods.description: 'Whole heaven' — primordial horizon. Father of Anu.

gods.name: Kishar
gods.description: 'Whole earth' — primordial horizon. Mother of Anu.

gods.name: Damgalnuna
gods.description: 'Great princess' — consort of Enki, mother of Marduk.

gods.name: Enkidu
gods.description: Wild man created by Aruru to rival Gilgamesh. Became his closest friend; his death set Gilgamesh on his quest for immortality.

gods.name: Ningal
gods.description: Moon-goddess consort of Nanna.

gods.name: Kingu
gods.description: Second consort of Tiamat; led her army. Marduk slew him and mixed his blood with clay to create humans.

gods.name: Sarpanit
gods.description: Consort of Marduk in Babylonian religion.

gods.name: Boann
gods.description: Goddess of the River Boyne. Approached Nechtan's forbidden well; it overflowed, drowning her and forming the river.

gods.name: Lir
gods.description: Sea-god; father of Manannán. In 'Children of Lir', his four children were cursed by jealous Aoife to spend 900 years as swans.

gods.name: Elatha
gods.description: Handsome Fomorian prince; father of Bres and (in some accounts) the Dagda himself.

gods.name: Bres
gods.description: Briefly king of the Tuatha Dé Danann after Nuada's maiming; oppressed his people; overthrown.

gods.name: Macha
gods.description: War-goddess; raced against Ulster's horses while pregnant and cursed the men of Ulster with labor-pains at need. One of the triple Morrígan.

gods.name: Badb
gods.description: Crow of battle; one of the triple Morrígan with Macha and Nemain (or the Morrígan herself).

gods.name: Epona
gods.description: Gallic horse-goddess. Uniquely for a Celtic deity, worshipped widely throughout the Roman Empire — patroness of cavalry.

gods.name: Cernunnos
gods.description: Antlered horned-god of the Gauls; depicted on the Gundestrup cauldron. Lord of wild nature and trade.

gods.name: Taranis
gods.description: Gallic thunder-god. Wheel-bearer. Equated by Romans with Jupiter.

gods.name: Centeotl
gods.description: Maize-god. Central to Aztec agricultural ritual.

gods.name: Ehecatl
gods.description: Wind-god; an aspect of Quetzalcoatl. Round temples were built for him because wind has no corners.

gods.name: Mayahuel
gods.description: Goddess of the maguey plant and pulque (fermented maguey sap). Many-breasted — nursed the 400 rabbit-gods of drunkenness.

gods.name: Tonantzin
gods.description: 'Our revered mother'. Associated with Tepeyac, where the apparition of Our Lady of Guadalupe was later reported in 1531.

gods.name: Mictecacíhuatl
gods.description: Queen of Mictlan. Her festival persists in modern Day of the Dead observances.

gods.name: Xolotl
gods.description: Dog-headed god; twin of Quetzalcoatl. Guides the sun through the underworld and escorts souls of the dead.

gods.name: Aether
gods.description: Pure upper atmosphere breathed by the gods.

gods.name: Hemera
gods.description: Personified day. Her mother Nyx left the sky as she arrived.

gods.name: Eurybia
gods.description: Sea-titaness of mastery over sea-power. Consort of the Titan Crius.

gods.name: Astraeus
gods.description: Titan of dusk; father of the winds and planets by Eos.

gods.name: Lilith (cross-pantheon)
gods.description: See main Lilith entry.

gods.name: Samael
gods.description: Ambiguous angel — both accuser and angel of death. Consort of Lilith in medieval Kabbalistic lore.

gods.name: Azazel
gods.description: Desert-figure to whom the Yom Kippur scapegoat was sent. In Enochic literature, a leader of fallen Watchers.

gods.name: Behemoth
gods.description: Primordial land-creature of Job 40. Paired in apocalyptic tradition with the sea-monster Leviathan.

gods.name: Leviathan
gods.description: Chaos-serpent of Job and the Psalms. Reflex of Canaanite Yam/Lotan. Eschatological banquet-food in rabbinic tradition.

gods.name: Perses
gods.description: Titan of destruction; husband of Asteria, father of Hecate.

gods.name: Asteria
gods.description: Titaness of falling stars; leapt into the sea to escape Zeus and became the island of Delos.

gods.name: Pallas
gods.description: Titan of battle; consort of Styx. Not to be confused with Pallas Athena.

gods.name: Caelus
gods.description: Roman personified sky; Uranus in Latin dress.

gods.name: Tellus
gods.description: Roman earth-mother; also called Terra.

gods.name: Aurora
gods.description: Roman dawn; Latin form of Eos.

gods.name: Proserpina
gods.description: Roman Persephone; queen of the underworld.

gods.name: Cupid
gods.description: Roman Eros. Winged archer whose arrows compel love.

gods.name: Sol
gods.description: Roman sun-god. Later elevated as Sol Invictus, state-sponsored from the 3rd century CE until displaced by Christianity.

gods.name: Luna
gods.description: Roman moon-goddess.

gods.name: Rhea Silvia
gods.description: Legendary vestal virgin; raped by Mars, bore Romulus and Remus.

gods.name: Remus
gods.description: Twin brother of Romulus. Killed by him over the naming of Rome.

gods.name: Latona
gods.description: Roman form of Leto; mother of Apollo and Diana.

gods.name: Fortuna
gods.description: Goddess of luck. Blindfolded; turns the wheel of fortune. Oracle at Praeneste.

gods.name: Bona Dea
gods.description: 'Good Goddess'. Rites performed only by women; males were strictly forbidden.

gods.name: Cybele
gods.description: Anatolian mother-goddess imported to Rome in 204 BCE. Her ecstatic rites included self-castrated priests (Galli).

gods.name: Attis
gods.description: Consort of Cybele. Castrated himself in madness; died and revived. Phrygian-origin dying-and-rising god.

gods.name: Mithras
gods.description: Roman mystery-god derived (loosely) from Iranian Mithra. Popular among Roman soldiers; worshipped in underground mithraea. Tauroctony (bull-slaying) is the core icon.

gods.name: Dioscuri
gods.description: Castor and Pollux — divine twin horsemen. One mortal (son of Tyndareus), one immortal (son of Zeus); Pollux shared his immortality. Constellation Gemini.

gods.name: Coronis
gods.description: Mother of Asclepius. Killed by Apollo for infidelity; her son cut from her corpse.

gods.name: Electryon
gods.description: King of Tiryns; son of Perseus, grandfather of Heracles.

gods.name: Medusa
gods.description: Only mortal of the three Gorgons. Slain by Perseus; her severed head retained its petrifying gaze.

gods.name: Melinoe
gods.description: Chthonic goddess of ghosts. Orphic tradition.

gods.name: Zagreus
gods.description: Orphic 'first Dionysus'. Dismembered by Titans; humanity arose from their ashes, carrying both Titan evil and Dionysian divinity.

gods.name: Hermaphroditus
gods.description: Merged with the nymph Salmacis into a single dual-sexed being. Eponym of hermaphrodite.

gods.name: Priapus
gods.description: Phallic fertility god; protector of gardens. Perpetually and grotesquely aroused.

gods.name: Hedone
gods.description: Personified pleasure. Source of the word 'hedonism'.

gods.name: Anemoi
gods.description: The four wind-gods: Boreas (north), Notus (south), Eurus (east), Zephyrus (west).

gods.name: Astra Planeta
gods.description: Five gods of the visible planets: Phainon (Saturn), Phaethon (Jupiter), Pyroeis (Mars), Eosphoros (Venus), Stilbon (Mercury).

gods.name: Electra
gods.description: Oceanid consort of Thaumas.

gods.name: Pleione
gods.description: Oceanid mother of the Pleiades.

gods.name: Perse
gods.description: Oceanid consort of Helios.

gods.name: Aeetes
gods.description: King of Colchis; keeper of the Golden Fleece. Father of Medea.

gods.name: Pasiphae
gods.description: Wife of Minos. Cursed by Poseidon to love a bull; mother of the Minotaur.

gods.name: Minotaur
gods.description: Bull-headed man confined in Daedalus's Labyrinth; fed on Athenian youths until slain by Theseus.

gods.name: Cyclopes
gods.description: One-eyed giants. Forged Zeus's thunderbolts, Poseidon's trident, Hades's helm.

gods.name: Hecatoncheires
gods.description: Hundred-handed fifty-headed giants. Imprisoned in Tartarus by Uranus, freed by Zeus in the Titanomachy.

gods.name: Oceanids
gods.description: Three thousand nymph-daughters of Oceanus and Tethys; presided over bodies of water.

gods.name: Nereids
gods.description: Fifty sea-nymph daughters of Nereus and Doris.

gods.name: Horae
gods.description: Goddesses of seasons and social order: Eunomia (good order), Dike (justice), Eirene (peace).

gods.name: Graces
gods.description: Three goddesses — Aglaea, Euphrosyne, Thalia. Attendants of Aphrodite; personify beauty, mirth, and good cheer.

gods.name: Menoetius
gods.description: Titan of violent rage. Struck down by Zeus in the Titanomachy.

gods.name: Pleiades
gods.description: Seven nymph-sisters: Maia, Alcyone, Celaeno, Electra, Sterope, Taygete, Merope. Placed among the stars as the Pleiades cluster.

gods.name: Daphne
gods.description: Naiad pursued by Apollo; transformed into a laurel tree to escape him.

gods.name: Orthus
gods.description: Two-headed dog; brother of Cerberus. Guarded Geryon's cattle; slain by Heracles.

gods.name: Sphinx
gods.description: Winged lion-woman who terrorized Thebes with her riddle. Destroyed herself when Oedipus solved it.

gods.name: Nemean Lion
gods.description: Invulnerable lion strangled by Heracles in his first labor. Wore its skin thereafter.

gods.name: Ladon
gods.description: Hundred-headed serpent guarding the golden apples of the Hesperides.

gods.name: Chrysaor
gods.description: Giant-warrior born from Medusa's neck alongside Pegasus.

gods.name: Nerthus
gods.description: Germanic earth-goddess described by Tacitus. May be sister-consort of Njörd.

gods.name: Fjörgyn
gods.description: Earth-goddess. Name is cognate with Baltic Perkūnas (PIE *Perkʷunos) — here a feminine earth, indicating the PIE pair was originally both.

gods.name: Nótt
gods.description: Personified night. Drives a horse-drawn chariot ahead of Dagr (day).

gods.name: Norns
gods.description: Urd (past), Verdandi (present), Skuld (future). Weave the fates of men and gods beside the well at Yggdrasil's roots.

gods.name: Gunnlöð
gods.description: Jötunn maiden; guardian of the mead of poetry. Seduced by Odin, who stole the mead.

gods.name: Rindr
gods.description: Mother of Váli by Odin — conceived to bear Baldr's avenger.

gods.name: Gríðr
gods.description: Friendly Jötunn; mother of Víðarr; lent Thor her staff and belt of strength.

gods.name: Asha
gods.description: One of the Amesha Spentas. 'Truth' and cosmic order.

gods.name: Amesha Spentas
gods.description: Six (or seven, with Spenta Mainyu) 'Bounteous Immortals' — personified aspects of Ahura Mazda: Vohu Manah, Asha, Khshathra, Armaiti, Haurvatat, Ameretat.

gods.name: Armaiti
gods.description: Amesha Spenta of devotion and the earth. Consort/daughter of Ahura Mazda.

gods.name: Horus the Elder
gods.description: Original sky-falcon Horus, sibling of Osiris. Later merged with Horus son of Isis.

gods.name: Nefertem
gods.description: Lotus-god; son of Ptah and Sekhmet. Dawn emerging from the primeval lotus.

gods.name: Naunet
gods.description: Female counterpart of Nun in the Hermopolitan Ogdoad.

gods.name: Four Sons of Horus
gods.description: Imsety, Hapi, Duamutef, and Qebehsenuef — guardians of the canopic jars holding organs of the mummified dead.

gods.name: Beli
gods.description: Welsh ancestral king; consort of Dôn. Founder-figure of Welsh royal genealogies.

gods.name: Manawydan
gods.description: Welsh sea-god. Cognate with Irish Manannán. Married Rhiannon after Pwyll's death.

gods.name: Llŷr
gods.description: Welsh sea-god. Cognate with Irish Lir; father of Brân, Branwen, and Manawydan.

gods.name: Tekawerahkwa
gods.description: Daughter of Sky-Woman; mother of the Twins. Died giving birth to Flint, who clawed out her side.

gods.name: Anansi
gods.description: Spider-trickster of the Akan and broader West African traditions. Folk tales traveled to the Americas; became the trickster of Caribbean and African-American folklore.

gods.name: Kotoamatsukami
gods.description: The first five 'separate heavenly kami' from whom later deities descended. Remote and unmanifest.

gods.name: Kotoamatsukami
gods.description: The 'separate heavenly deities' — first five kami who appeared at the beginning.

gods.name: El (Semitic supreme)
gods.description: Semitic root-word for 'god'. Cognate across Canaanite El, early Israelite Elohim, Arabian Il, and later Allāh.

gods.name: Lugalbanda
gods.description: Legendary king of Uruk and second king of the Sumerian King List's post-flood dynasty. Deified; father of Gilgamesh.

gods.name: Ninsun
gods.description: 'Wild Cow' goddess; mother of Gilgamesh. Counseled her son through the Epic.

gods.name: Phaedra
gods.description: Sister of Ariadne, wife of Theseus. Fell fatally in love with her stepson Hippolytus — subject of tragedies by Euripides, Seneca, and Racine.

gods.name: Doris
gods.description: Oceanid consort of Nereus; mother of the fifty Nereids.

gods.name: Styx
gods.description: Oceanid river-goddess of the underworld. The gods swore unbreakable oaths on her waters.

gods.name: Tithonus
gods.description: Trojan prince loved by Eos. Granted immortality but not eternal youth; shriveled into a cicada.

gods.name: Memnon
gods.description: King of Ethiopia who fought for Troy; slain by Achilles. Eos won posthumous honors for him from Zeus.

gods.name: Orthrus
gods.description: Two-headed hound of Geryon; slain by Heracles.

gods.name: Alcaeus
gods.description: Son of Perseus. Grandfather of Heracles through Amphitryon.

gods.name: Dryope
gods.description: Oread/wood-nymph; mother of Pan by Hermes. Transformed into a lotus-tree.

gods.name: Alcis
gods.description: Divine twin-gods of the Naharvali (Germanic) worshipped in a sacred grove, per Tacitus. Direct continuation of the PIE Divine Twins.

gods.name: Parcae
gods.description: Roman Fates: Nona, Decima, Morta. Cognate roles to Greek Moirai.

gods.name: Larunda
gods.description: Silent nymph mother of the Lares; punished by Jupiter for gossip by having her tongue cut out.

gods.name: Latinus
gods.description: Legendary king of the Latins; gave his daughter Lavinia in marriage to Aeneas.

gods.name: Picus
gods.description: Italian woodpecker-god of augury. Transformed by Circe when he refused her.

gods.name: Sarruma
gods.description: Son of Teshub/Tarhunt and Hebat. Mountain-god, depicted riding a panther.

gods.name: Potamoi
gods.description: River-gods; three thousand sons of Oceanus and Tethys. Each river had its own Potamos.
